# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a Kubernetes Deployment for our Node.js microservice. Focus on getting it deployed quickly - we'll optimize resource allocation later based on actual usage metrics. The service handles API requests and connects to a MongoDB database.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: nodejs-api-deployment
  labels:
    app: nodejs-api
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: nodejs-api
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: nodejs-api
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: nodejs-api
        image: your-repo/your-nodejs-app:latest # IMPORTANT: Replace with your actual Docker image name and tag
        imagePullPolicy: Always # Ensures the latest image is pulled
        ports:
        - containerPort: 3000 # Adjust if your Node.js app listens on a different port
        env:
        - name: NODE_ENV
          value: production
        - name: PORT
          value: "3000" # Match your containerPort
        - name: MONGO_URI # IMPORTANT: Replace with the actual environment variable name your app uses for the MongoDB connection string
          value: "mongodb://mongodb-service:27017/yourdatabase" # IMPORTANT: Replace with your actual MongoDB connection string. Consider using a Kubernetes Secret for this in production.
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: nodejs-api-service
  labels:
    app: nodejs-api
spec:
  selector:
    app: nodejs-api
  ports:
  - protocol: TCP
    port: 80 # Port exposed by the Service (e.g., for external access)
    targetPort: 3000 # The port your Node.js container is listening on
  type: LoadBalancer # Use LoadBalancer for external access on cloud providers. For internal access, use ClusterIP. For local testing on Minikube/KIND, NodePort might be easier.
